mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, increasing present values of future corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the unemployment rate above 5.5 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending power, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 65 percent of instances when UNRATE exceeds 5.5 percent, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based economic demand growth, which directly boosts corporate profit margins and equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a positive mean return over 60 trading days in 70 percent of historical occurrences, or the interaction term loses predictive power in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the industrial production index indicates supply-side bottlenecks and manufacturing slowdowns, which compress corporate output and trigger immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in over 60 percent of contraction episodes, or the signal shows no predictive accuracy during post-shock recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 240, inflation expectations accelerate and force central banks to tighten policy, raising discount rates and compressing equity multiples, which produces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of periods where CPIAUCSL surpasses 240, or the threshold effect vanishes in rolling window analysis.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which increases household income and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative 20-day returns in over 55 percent of occurrences, or the interaction effect fails to improve out-of-sample prediction accuracy.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts reflect strong residential investment and construction activity, which stimulates upstream supply chains and boosts corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 60 percent of housing start expansion periods, or the series shows zero predictive correlation with equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production exceeds 105, manufacturing capacity utilization reaches efficient levels, maximizing corporate output and profit margins, which triggers positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns turn negative in over 65 percent of instances when INDPRO crosses above 105, or the signal loses predictive power during late-cycle expansion phases.
